rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tri-/difluoromethylation-triggered radical cyclization of N-substituted 2-aryl indole acrylamides coupled with oxidative cleavage of the indole C(2)=C(3) bond to deliver fluoroalkylated 2-(2-acetylphenyl)isoquinoline-1,3-diones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (or Mn(II)/CF2HSO2Na for CF2H) generates the fluoroalkyl radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene; subsequent anodic oxidations advance the cyclized intermediate to a radical cation and then to an iminium, while the cathode reduces water/H+ to H2 generating hydroxide that delivers the two new C=O oxygens.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (loss of SO2)
Carbon radical A from CF3 addition to acrylamide alkene
Cyclohexadienyl-type radical B after radical cyclization onto indole C2
Radical cation D from anodic oxidation of 6
C3-hydroxylated intermediate E (iminium after OH addition)
trans-2,3-dihydroxy intermediate F after second OH addition at C2
Alkoxide/diol G prior to C-C cleavage
Ring-opened intermediate H after C2-C3 bond cleavage

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- (or Mn(II) for CF2H) to generate a fluoroalkyl radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene and cyclizes onto the indole; a second anodic oxidation/deprotonation gives the closed indoloisoquinoline 6, which is further oxidized at the anode to a radical cation, then sequentially attacked by cathodically generated hydroxide at C3 and C2 to give a trans-diol; deprotonation and C2–C3 bond cleavage afford the diketone product while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely quenched; TEMPO–CF3 and TEMPO–CF2H adducts detected, supporting fluoroalkyl radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Isolated cyclization intermediate 6
probe_reagents.observation: 6 isolated in 65% after 2 h electrolysis and converted to product 3a in 93% under standard conditions, establishing 6 as a key on-cycle intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: N-Boc 2-aryl indole 1r (no acrylamide tether)
probe_reagents.observation: Without Langlois reagent gave indole oxidative cleavage product 7 in 71%, showing the oxidative cleavage step is independent of fluoroalkylation.

probe_reagents.name: H2O-18 isotope labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Doubly 18O-labeled product 3f detected by HRMS, confirming both new carbonyl oxygens originate from water.

probe_reagents.name: N2 atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction under N2 still gave 3a in 76%, ruling out O2 as the oxygen source.

probe_reagents.name: Anhydrous conditions
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product formed without water (Table 1 entry 11), consistent with H2O as O source.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 2b
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(CF3SO2Na)=1.1 V and Eox(CF2HSO2Na)=0.8 V vs Ag/AgCl, both well below Eox(1a)=1.58 V, so fluoroalkyl precursors are oxidized first.

notes: For the CF2H variant a MnCl2·4H2O co-catalyst is required: anodic oxidation of Mn(II) to a Mn(III)-CF2H species delivers the CF2H radical, while the CF3 pathway is metal-free. Authors explicitly establish the sequence as fluoroalkylation → cyclization → indole C(2)=C(3) oxidative cleavage (not the reverse order).
